alternatives.name: Near-net-shape precision cold-drawn / cold-rolled profile stock
alternatives.tradeoffs: Custom tooling lead 6-12 weeks; min 100 kg economic batch; 2D constant cross-section only; die ownership complication at program end; higher unit cost vs. bar stock; IT8 tolerance (+-0.02 mm); in-line IH available [SRC: JTBD_automotive_components]

alternatives.name: Standard round / flat bar stock machined at Tier 1
alternatives.tradeoffs: 40-80% material waste at Tier 1; machining cycle time consumes Tier 1 CNC capacity; no integrated surface hardness; zero tooling cost; commodity sourcing from multiple distributors; ~53% market share [SRC: VN_automotive_components]

alternatives.name: Cold roll-formed structural open section
alternatives.tradeoffs: Tolerance +-0.5 mm inadequate for IT8 bearing surfaces; limited cross-section complexity; no in-line IH; cost-efficient at volume; suitable only for structural (OT3) not precision (OT1/OT4); ~25% share [SRC: JTBD_automotive_components]

alternatives.name: Hot-stamped / press-hardened section (22MnB5)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Ultra-high strength 1,500 MPa; uniform hardness; die lead 12-20+ weeks; complex process PPAP; limited capacity; higher per-unit cost; ~12% share growing at 8% CAGR [SRC: ADJ_automotive_components]

alternatives.name: Do nothing — continue machining from bar stock (status quo)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero P1 burden; 40-80% machining waste embedded in Tier 1 unit cost; no new qualification; no tooling investment; most common competitor in all segments
